title: commelina anomala
intro: Commelina anomala, also known as small blue dayflower or wandering Jew, is a type of flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Commelina anomala include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of this plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
